event: A church building in the village of Labastide
symbols: church steeple — the upward aspiration of prayer and the visible presence of the Church in a community
reflected image in water — the heavenly reality mirrored in the earthly, the Church as image of the heavenly Jerusalem
warm evening light — the golden hour evoking peace, contemplation, and the approaching rest of vespers
trees flanking the church — the tree of life and the sheltering presence of God's planting
description: A pointillist painting depicts a stone church with a tall pointed steeple and bell tower rising above surrounding trees in a French village. The church facade features a rose window or circular detail and Gothic-influenced arched elements. Warm golden and violet light of late afternoon or evening suffuses the scene. A tall slender tree, likely a poplar, stands to the left. In the foreground, a still pond or stream reflects the church, steeple, and trees in a shimmering mirror image. Smaller buildings adjoin the church.
